questions.q: What is the general structure of aldehydes?
questions.answer: Aldehydes have the general structure RCHO, where R is an alkyl group.

questions.q: State the reaction of aldehydes with Tollen's reagent.
questions.answer: Aldehydes reduce Tollen's reagent to a silver mirror.
key_points: Reaction of aldehydes with Tollen's reagent

questions.q: Give an example of a ketone.
questions.answer: Acetone (CH3COCH3) is an example of a ketone.

questions.q: Explain the difference between aldehydes and ketones.
questions.answer: Aldehydes have a carbonyl group at the end of the carbon chain, while ketones have a carbonyl group within the carbon chain. Aldehydes are more reactive than ketones.

questions.q: What is the general structure of carboxylic acids?
questions.answer: Carboxylic acids have the general structure RCOOH, where R is an alkyl group.

questions.q: State the reaction of carboxylic acids with sodium bicarbonate.
questions.answer: Carboxylic acids react with sodium bicarbonate to produce carbon dioxide, water, and a salt.
key_points: Reaction of carboxylic acids with sodium bicarbonate

questions.q: Explain the preparation of aldehydes from alkyl halides.
questions.answer: Aldehydes can be prepared from alkyl halides by reaction with zinc cyanide to form nitriles, followed by hydrolysis to form aldehydes.
key_points: Preparation of aldehydes from alkyl halides

questions.q: Give an example of a carboxylic acid derivative.
questions.answer: Esters (RCOOR') are examples of carboxylic acid derivatives.
key_points: Example of a carboxylic acid derivative

questions.q: Explain the difference between esters and anhydrides.
questions.answer: Esters have a hydroxyl group replaced by an alkoxy group, while anhydrides have two carboxylic acid groups joined together. Esters are more stable than anhydrides.

questions.q: What is the general structure of carboxylic anhydrides?
questions.answer: Carboxylic anhydrides have the general structure RCOO-CO-R, where R is an alkyl group.

questions.q: State the reaction of carboxylic acids with Grignard reagents.
questions.answer: Carboxylic acids react with Grignard reagents to form ketones.
key_points: Reaction of carboxylic acids with Grignard reagents

questions.q: Explain the preparation of carboxylic acids from alkenes.
questions.answer: Carboxylic acids can be prepared from alkenes by ozonolysis, followed by hydrolysis to form carboxylic acids.
key_points: Preparation of carboxylic acids from alkenes

questions.q: Give an example of a reaction of aldehydes with ammonia.
questions.answer: Aldehydes react with ammonia to form imines.

questions.q: Explain the preparation of esters from carboxylic acids.
questions.answer: Esters can be prepared from carboxylic acids by reaction with alcohols in the presence of an acid catalyst.
key_points: Preparation of esters from carboxylic acids

questions.q: State the reaction of aldehydes with sodium hydroxide.
questions.answer: Aldehydes react with sodium hydroxide to form sodium salts and water.
key_points: Reaction of aldehydes with sodium hydroxide

questions.q: Explain the preparation of carboxylic acids from alcohols.
questions.answer: Carboxylic acids can be prepared from alcohols by oxidation with potassium dichromate.
key_points: Preparation of carboxylic acids from alcohols
